description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel installation, featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels supported by a single vertical metal pole. The solar panel array consists of six individual modules arranged in a 2x3 grid, each with a dark, glassy surface and visible cell patterns. The supporting pole is anchored into a large, rectangular concrete base that sits atop a patch of textured grass or earth, providing stability and resistance to wind loads. The panel array is tilted at an angle, optimizing exposure to sunlight, and is framed with metallic structural elements for rigidity. The rear of the panel shows mounting brackets and support struts, indicating a robust and weather-resistant design. No visible electrical cabling or inverter housing is apparent from the exterior views.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with six modules on a metal pole anchored in concrete, set on a grassy base. The panels are tilted and framed with metallic supports.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the metallic frame, support struts, and the concrete base embedded in grass.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angle of the solar panel array and the vertical support pole.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the six-panel grid, dark photovoltaic surfaces, and the supporting structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the tilt and the robust mounting structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, ground installation with a steel or aluminum frame and concrete base
attribute_array_configuration: 2x3 grid of solar panels in a single rectangular array
